original_abstract: Motivated by the recent synthesis of $X\text{B}_3\text{C}_3$ ($X = \text{Sr}, \text{La}$) in bipartite sodalite clathrate structures, we employ density functional theory (DFT) to explore this chemical family, including derivatives containing two distinct metal species. A DFT-chemical pressure analysis of systems where $X = \text{Mg}, \text{Ca}, \text{Sr}, \text{Ba}$ demonstrates that the size of the metal cation is a critical factor for ambient-pressure stability, as the guest must be properly sized to support the B–C framework. High-throughput screening of 105 binary-guest $XY\text{B}_6\text{C}_6$ compounds ($Pm\bar{3}$ symmetry) identified 22 dynamically stable candidates at 1 atm, significantly expanding the pool of potentially realizable phases by 19 (comprising 18 metals and 1 insulator). Our results show that the electronic density of states at the Fermi level and the superconducting transition temperature ($T_c$) can be effectively tuned by adjusting the average oxidation state of the metal atoms, with $T_c$ peaking at an average valence of +1.5. Among all studied $Pm\bar{3}n$ $X\text{B}_3\text{C}_3$ and $Pm\bar{3}$ $XY\text{B}_6\text{C}_6$ phases, $\text{KPbB}_6\text{C}_6$ stands out with a predicted Eliashberg $T_c$ of 88 K at ambient pressure. Further analysis suggests that $\text{KPbB}_6\text{C}_6$ is a viable target for high-pressure, high-temperature synthesis followed by quenching to ambient conditions.
humanized_abstract: Motivated by our recent discovery of synthesis of X B 3 C 3 (X= Sr, La) compounds in bipartite sodalite clathrate structures, we used density functional theory (DFT) to study the chemistry of this group of compounds including the derivatives of mixed metal composition. The DFT-chemical pressure analysis of Mg-B-C, Ca-B-C, Sr-B-C and Ba-B-C systems shows that the size of the divalent metal cation has large effects on the structural stability of X B 3 C 3 -type compounds and that an appropriate metal size is necessary for the structural stability of these compounds at ambient pressure. High-throughput screening (HTS) of 105 binary-guest XYB 6 C 6 compounds with Pm 3 ( Pm 3 ̄) symmetry found that 22 compounds are dynamically stable at 1 atm. The studies significantly expand the pool of possible synthesis compounds by 19 that are consisting of 18 different metals and 1 insulator. Importantly, the electronic density of states (DOS) at the Fermi level and the superconducting transition temperature (T c ) in XYB 6 C 6 compounds can be controlled within certain ranges by adjusting the average valence of the mixed metal composition and the T c maximizes at average valence of +1.5. The highest predicted Eliashberg T c is 88 K at 1 atm found for KPB6C6. Thus, KPBB6C6 seems to be an optimal candidate which might be synthesized by high pressure and high temperature and then quenched down to ambient pressure.

document.input: Motivated by the recent synthesis of $X\text{B}_3\text{C}_3$ ($X = \text{Sr}, \text{La}$) in bipartite sodalite clathrate structures, we employ density functional theory (DFT) to explore this chemical family, including derivatives containing two distinct metal species. Our results show that the electronic density of states at the Fermi level and the superconducting transition temperature ($T_c$) can be effectively tuned by adjusting the average oxidation state of the metal atoms, with $T_c$ peaking at an average valence of +1.5. Further analysis suggests that $\text{KPbB}_6\text{C}_6$ is a viable target for high-pressure, high-temperature synthesis followed by quenching to ambient conditions.
